questions.text: Which of the following is sufficient to construct a quadrilateral using ruler and compass?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral can be constructed if 4 sides and 1 diagonal are given, as this fixes the shape uniquely.

questions.text: To construct a parallelogram, which minimum data is required?
questions.explanation: A parallelogram can be constructed with two adjacent sides and the included angle between them.

questions.text: Which tool is NOT used in constructions of quadrilaterals?
questions.explanation: Only compass and ruler are allowed for constructions; protractor is not used.

questions.text: How many sides and diagonals are needed to construct a quadrilateral if 3 sides and 2 diagonals are given?
questions.explanation: Given 3 sides and 2 diagonals is sufficient to construct a quadrilateral.

questions.text: Which quadrilateral can be constructed if 4 sides and 1 angle are given?
questions.explanation: A general quadrilateral can be constructed if 4 sides and 1 angle are given.

questions.text: To construct a rectangle using compass and ruler, minimum data required is:
questions.explanation: A rectangle can be constructed with two adjacent sides and the right angle between them.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a special quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: Pentagon is a polygon with 5 sides, not a quadrilateral.

questions.text: What is the minimum data needed to construct a rhombus?
questions.explanation: All sides of a rhombus are equal, so 4 sides (equal length) are sufficient.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about the diagonals of a square?

options.a: They are equal and bisect each other at right angles
options.c: They do not bisect each other
options.d: They are equal but do not bisect at right angles

questions.explanation: Square's diagonals are equal in length and bisect each other at right angles.

questions.text: When constructing a quadrilateral with 3 sides and 2 included angles, what is the key step?

options.a: Draw one side and measure angles at its endpoints

questions.explanation: Drawing one side and measuring the given included angles at its endpoints helps locate the other vertices.

questions.text: Which of the following data sets is NOT sufficient to construct a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: 2 sides and 2 angles are not sufficient to fix a unique quadrilateral.

questions.text: Which special quadrilateral has all sides equal and all angles equal?
questions.explanation: A square has all sides equal and all angles 90 degrees.

questions.text: In construction, the compass is used to:

options.b: Measure and draw arcs or circles

questions.explanation: Compass is used to draw arcs and circles, essential in constructions.

questions.text: Which is true about the diagonals of a rhombus?

options.b: They bisect each other at right angles
options.c: They do not bisect each other
options.d: They are unequal and do not bisect

questions.explanation: Rhombus diagonals bisect each other at right angles but are not equal.

questions.text: To construct a quadrilateral with 4 sides and 1 diagonal, what is the first step?
questions.explanation: Drawing the diagonal first helps fix the shape by connecting two vertices.

questions.text: Which of the following quadrilaterals can be constructed if 3 sides and 2 diagonals are given?
questions.explanation: A general quadrilateral can be constructed with 3 sides and 2 diagonals.

questions.text: When constructing a parallelogram, why is the knowledge of one angle important?

options.a: To fix the length of sides
options.b: To fix the shape uniquely by setting the angle between sides

questions.explanation: The angle between adjacent sides fixes the shape uniquely in a parallelogram.

questions.text: Which quadrilateral has diagonals that are equal and bisect each other but are NOT perpendicular?
questions.explanation: Rectangle's diagonals are equal and bisect each other but are not perpendicular.

questions.text: If you have 2 adjacent sides and 2 included angles of a quadrilateral, what can you construct?
questions.explanation: Two adjacent sides and two included angles fix the shape uniquely for construction.

questions.text: Which of the following is a correct sequence in constructing a quadrilateral given 4 sides and 1 angle?

options.a: Draw one side, construct the given angle, then draw adjacent sides
options.c: Draw the angle first, then sides

questions.explanation: Drawing one side first, then constructing the given angle at its endpoint, helps in accurate construction.

questions.text: Ravi wants to construct a quadrilateral plot of land with sides 60 m, 40 m, 50 m and 30 m, and one diagonal of 70 m. Which of the following is the correct first step in constructing this quadrilateral using compass and ruler?

options.a: Draw a line segment of 70 m representing the diagonal.
options.b: Draw a line segment of 60 m representing one side.
options.c: Draw a line segment of 50 m representing one side.
options.d: Draw a line segment of 30 m representing one side.

questions.explanation: When constructing a quadrilateral given four sides and one diagonal, the diagonal is drawn first as the base. Then arcs are drawn from the ends to locate other vertices.

questions.text: A rectangular garden has length 80 m and width 50 m. To construct this rectangle using compass and ruler, which minimum data is required?
questions.explanation: To construct a rectangle, length, width and one right angle are sufficient. The right angle ensures the shape is a rectangle.

questions.text: An architect is constructing a rhombus-shaped window with each side 40 cm and one angle 60°. How should the architect start the construction?

options.a: Draw a side of 40 cm and construct a 60° angle at one end.
options.c: Draw two adjacent sides of 40 cm each at 90° angle.

questions.explanation: A rhombus has all sides equal. Drawing one side and then constructing the given angle at one end helps in locating the next vertex.

questions.text: A plot has three sides measuring 70 m, 50 m, 40 m and two diagonals measuring 60 m and 80 m. Which data should be drawn first to start constructing this quadrilateral?

options.a: Draw the diagonal of 80 m first.
options.b: Draw the side of 70 m first.
options.c: Draw the diagonal of 60 m first.
options.d: Draw the side of 50 m first.

questions.explanation: When given three sides and two diagonals, the longer diagonal is usually drawn first to serve as the base for construction.

questions.text: A carpenter needs to make a square table with side 1.2 m. Which of the following is the minimum data he needs to construct it using compass and ruler?

options.a: One side and one angle of 90°.
options.c: Two adjacent sides and one diagonal.

questions.explanation: A square has all sides equal and all angles 90°. Knowing one side and one right angle is sufficient for construction.

questions.text: A farmer wants to construct a parallelogram field with adjacent sides 120 m and 80 m and the included angle 60°. What is the correct sequence to start the construction?

options.a: Draw side 120 m, then construct 60° angle and draw side 80 m.
options.b: Draw side 80 m, then construct 90° angle and draw side 120 m.
options.d: Draw side 120 m and side 80 m at 90° angle.

questions.explanation: To construct a parallelogram with given adjacent sides and included angle, draw one side, then construct the given angle, then draw the adjacent side.

questions.text: A designer is constructing a quadrilateral with sides 25 cm, 30 cm, 35 cm, 40 cm and one diagonal of 45 cm. Which tool is essential to locate the vertices after drawing the diagonal?

options.b: Compass to draw arcs from diagonal ends.

questions.explanation: Compass is used to draw arcs from ends of diagonal with lengths equal to sides to locate vertices accurately.

questions.text: A rectangle has length 15 m and width 10 m. If the diagonal is drawn first, what will be its length?
questions.explanation: Diagonal length = √(length² + width²) = √(15² + 10²) = √(225 + 100) = √325 ≈ 18.03 m, so closest is 18 m (option b). Correction: option b is 18 m, which is correct, so correct answer is b.

questions.text: A rhombus has side 10 cm and one diagonal 16 cm. Which of the following is true about the other diagonal?
questions.explanation: In a rhombus, diagonals bisect each other at right angles. Using Pythagoras theorem: half diagonals are x and 8 cm, and side is 10 cm. So, x² + 8² = 10² ⇒ x² = 100 - 64 = 36 ⇒ x = 6 cm. Other diagonal = 2x = 12 cm.

questions.text: A quadrilateral has three sides 50 m, 40 m, 30 m and two included angles 60° and 90°. Which data should be drawn first to construct it?

options.a: Draw side 50 m and construct 60° angle at one end.
options.b: Draw side 30 m and construct 90° angle at one end.
options.c: Draw side 40 m and construct 60° angle at one end.
options.d: Draw side 50 m and construct 90° angle at one end.

questions.explanation: Start by drawing the longest side and constructing one of the given included angles at its end to locate the next vertex.

questions.text: You are given a quadrilateral ABCD where AB = 6 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 7 cm, DA = 4 cm and diagonal AC = 8 cm. Using only a ruler and compass, which of the following is the correct first step to construct this quadrilateral?

options.a: Draw diagonal AC of length 8 cm.
options.b: Draw side AB of length 6 cm.
options.c: Draw side BC of length 5 cm.
options.d: Draw side CD of length 7 cm.

questions.explanation: Since the diagonal AC is given along with all four sides, the first step is to draw the diagonal AC of length 8 cm. This will serve as the base to construct triangles ABC and ADC using the given sides.

questions.text: Given a quadrilateral PQRS with sides PQ = 7 cm, QR = 5 cm, RS = 6 cm, SP = 8 cm and diagonals PR = 9 cm, QS = 10 cm, which two triangles should you construct first to complete the quadrilateral?

options.a: Triangles PQR and PRS using diagonal PR.
options.b: Triangles PQS and QRS using diagonal QS.
options.c: Triangles PQR and PQS using side PQ.
options.d: Triangles PRS and PQS using side PS.

questions.explanation: The diagonals PR and QS divide the quadrilateral into four triangles. Since PR is given, constructing triangles PQR and PRS first using diagonal PR helps in accurate construction of the quadrilateral.

questions.text: You need to construct a parallelogram ABCD given AB = 6 cm, BC = 4 cm and angle ABC = 60°. What is the sequence of steps to construct this parallelogram?

options.a: Draw AB = 6 cm, construct angle ABC = 60°, then draw BC = 4 cm and complete parallelogram using parallel lines.
options.b: Draw BC = 4 cm, construct angle ABC = 60°, then draw AB = 6 cm and complete the figure.
options.c: Draw AB = 6 cm, draw BC = 4 cm, then construct angle ABC = 60° and complete parallelogram.
options.d: Draw AB = 6 cm, construct angle BAD = 60°, then draw BC = 4 cm and complete parallelogram.

questions.explanation: Start by drawing AB = 6 cm. At point B, construct angle ABC = 60°, then draw BC = 4 cm along this angle. Finally, draw lines parallel to AB and BC through points C and A respectively to complete the parallelogram.

questions.text: Construct a rhombus with side 5 cm and one diagonal 8 cm. Which of the following is true about the diagonals in this construction?

options.a: Diagonals bisect each other at right angles, so construct one diagonal first and then draw the other perpendicular bisector.
options.b: Diagonals are equal, so draw both diagonals of 8 cm each.
options.c: Diagonals are parallel, so draw two diagonals parallel to each other.
options.d: Diagonals do not bisect each other, so draw sides first.

questions.explanation: In a rhombus, diagonals bisect each other at right angles. Given one diagonal, draw it first, then construct the other diagonal perpendicular to it, bisecting it, to complete the rhombus.

questions.text: You are given three sides and two included angles of a quadrilateral: AB = 6 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 7 cm, angle ABC = 60°, and angle BCD = 90°. What is the correct approach to construct this quadrilateral?

options.a: Draw AB = 6 cm, construct angle ABC = 60°, draw BC = 5 cm, construct angle BCD = 90°, then draw CD = 7 cm and join DA.
options.b: Draw BC = 5 cm, construct angle ABC = 60°, draw AB = 6 cm, construct angle BCD = 90°, then draw CD = 7 cm and join DA.
options.c: Draw CD = 7 cm, construct angle BCD = 90°, draw BC = 5 cm, construct angle ABC = 60°, then draw AB = 6 cm and join DA.
options.d: Draw AB = 6 cm, draw BC = 5 cm, draw CD = 7 cm, then construct angles ABC and BCD.

questions.explanation: Start with AB = 6 cm. At B, construct angle ABC = 60°, then draw BC = 5 cm. At C, construct angle BCD = 90°, then draw CD = 7 cm. Finally, join D to A to complete the quadrilateral.

questions.text: To construct a square ABCD using only a side length of 5 cm, which of the following sequences correctly describes the construction steps?

options.a: Draw AB = 5 cm, construct perpendicular at A and B, mark points D and C at 5 cm on these perpendiculars, then join CD and DA.
options.b: Draw AB = 5 cm, draw diagonal AC = 5√2 cm, construct perpendicular bisector of AB, then mark points C and D.
options.c: Draw AB = 5 cm, construct angle BAD = 90°, draw BC = 5 cm, then join CD and DA.
options.d: Draw AB = 5 cm, construct angle ABC = 45°, draw BC = 5 cm, then join CD and DA.

questions.explanation: Draw AB = 5 cm. At points A and B, construct perpendicular lines. On these perpendiculars, mark points D and C respectively at 5 cm. Join CD and DA to complete the square.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral ABCD given that AB = 5 cm, BC = 6 cm, CD = 4 cm, DA = 7 cm and diagonal AC = 8 cm. Find the length of diagonal BD.
questions.explanation: Using the given sides and diagonal AC, construct the quadrilateral and then measure diagonal BD using ruler and compass.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral ABCD with AB = 6 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 7 cm, and diagonals AC = 8 cm, BD = 9 cm. Calculate the length of side DA.
questions.explanation: After constructing the quadrilateral with given sides and diagonals, measure side DA using ruler and compass.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral PQRS with sides PQ = 5 cm, QR = 6 cm, RS = 5 cm, SP = 6 cm and angle P = 90°. Find the length of diagonal QS.
questions.explanation: Using the given sides and right angle at P, construct the quadrilateral and measure diagonal QS.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral WXYZ with sides WX = 7 cm, XY = 5 cm, YZ = 6 cm, ZX = 8 cm and angle X = 60°. Find the length of diagonal WY.
questions.explanation: Construct the quadrilateral with given sides and angle, then measure diagonal WY.

questions.text: Construct a parallelogram ABCD where AB = 6 cm, BC = 4 cm and angle A = 60°. Find the length of diagonal AC.
questions.explanation: Using properties of parallelogram and given data, construct and measure diagonal AC.

questions.text: Construct a rhombus PQRS with side 5 cm and one diagonal PR = 8 cm. Find the length of the other diagonal QS.
questions.explanation: Since diagonals of rhombus bisect each other at right angles, use Pythagoras theorem to find QS after construction.

questions.text: Construct a rectangle ABCD with length AB = 8 cm and diagonal AC = 10 cm. Find the breadth BC.
questions.explanation: Using Pythagoras theorem in rectangle, breadth = sqrt(diagonal² - length²).

questions.text: Construct a square PQRS with side 6 cm. Find the length of diagonal PR.
questions.explanation: Diagonal of square = side × √2 = 6 × 1.414 = 8.49 cm (approx).

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral ABCD with sides AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 6 cm, DA = 4 cm and angles B = 90°, D = 90°. Find the length of diagonal AC.
questions.explanation: Construct the quadrilateral with given sides and right angles, then measure diagonal AC.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral EFGH with sides EF = 6 cm, FG = 7 cm, GH = 5 cm, HE = 4 cm and included angles at F and G as 60° and 90° respectively. Find the length of diagonal EG.
questions.explanation: Using given sides and angles, construct the quadrilateral and measure diagonal EG.

questions.case_text: Ravi is designing a rectangular garden in his backyard. He wants to construct a parallelogram-shaped flower bed inside the garden using wooden planks. He has the lengths of all four sides of the parallelogram and one diagonal measurement. He plans to use only a compass and ruler to construct the flower bed accurately.

subparts.text: What minimum data should Ravi use to construct the parallelogram flower bed accurately using compass and ruler?
subparts.explanation: To construct a parallelogram with compass and ruler, knowing all four sides and one diagonal is sufficient to determine the shape accurately.

subparts.text: If Ravi knows the lengths of all four sides but no diagonal, which of the following constructions is NOT possible using only compass and ruler?

options.c: Constructing a parallelogram with given sides

subparts.explanation: Without a diagonal or angle, constructing a unique quadrilateral with just four sides is not possible because multiple quadrilaterals can have the same four side lengths.

subparts.text: Ravi measures the sides of the parallelogram as 8 m, 5 m, 8 m, and 5 m, and one diagonal as 9 m. What type of parallelogram is he constructing?
subparts.explanation: Since opposite sides are equal and the diagonal is longer than the sides, and the sides are not all equal, the parallelogram with given data is a rectangle.

questions.case_text: Sneha is helping her school prepare a square-shaped stage for an annual function. She has a wooden plank of length 6 m and needs to construct the square stage using only a compass and ruler. She also needs to mark the angles accurately to ensure the stage is perfectly square.

subparts.text: Which minimum data should Sneha use to construct the square stage accurately?
subparts.explanation: To construct a square, knowing all four sides equal and one right angle is sufficient to construct it accurately.

subparts.text: If Sneha only knows the length of one side (6 m) and no angles, can she construct the square using compass and ruler?

options.a: Yes, because all sides are equal in a square
options.b: No, because angle measurement is required
options.c: Yes, if she knows the diagonal
options.d: No, because she needs at least 3 sides

subparts.explanation: Knowing only one side length is not sufficient; the right angle must be constructed to ensure the shape is a square.

subparts.text: Sneha constructs the square stage with each side 6 m. What is the length of the diagonal of the square? (in meters)
subparts.explanation: The diagonal d = side × √2 = 6 × 1.414 = 8.49 m approximately.
